text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, status post right radical nephrectomy and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. Currently on cabozantinib 40 mg orally daily (second-line therapy following progression on ipilimumab/nivolumab). Presenting for routine restaging evaluation. History of sarcomatoid differentiation (20%), MET amplification. Recent complaint of low back pain, now attributed to degenerative lumbar disc disease per separate MRI lumbar spine. This examination serves as the 9-month restaging scan on cabozantinib.

COMPARISON: CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast (6-month restaging scan on cabozantinib, approximately 3 months prior). Prior CT at cabozantinib initiation also reviewed for longitudinal assessment.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis acquired in the portal venous phase following the intravenous administration of 100 mL of iohexol contrast (no adverse reaction). Lung windows, soft tissue windows, and bone windows reviewed. Maximum intensity projection (MIP) reconstructions reviewed for pulmonary nodule assessment. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformats reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the level of the subsegmental bronchi bilaterally. No endobronchial lesions. No bronchiectasis.

Pulmonary Parenchyma:
- Right lower lobe dominant pulmonary nodule (previously measured 2.1 cm on prior 6-month cabozantinib restaging scan): now measures approximately 2.0 cm in greatest axial dimension (series 3, image 44). This nodule has shown continued slow, gradual interval decrease since cabozantinib initiation, when it measured 2.6 cm. The nodule remains solid, well-circumscribed, and without new internal characteristics. No cavitation. No pleural tethering. Compared to the original pre-treatment baseline when it measured 1.8 cm and subsequently grew to 2.6 cm on nivolumab, the current measurement reflects an overall net reduction from the peak size. The continued slow downward trajectory on cabozantinib is maintained.
- Right lower lobe second pulmonary nodule: measures 0.4 cm (series 3, image 51), grossly stable compared with 0.4 cm on prior scan. No significant change.
- Left lower lobe pulmonary nodule: measures 0.3 cm (series 4, image 38), representing a minimal decrease from 0.4 cm previously. This nodule continues its slow favorable trajectory.
- Right middle lobe nodule 1 (previously 0.4 cm): now measures 0.3 cm (series 3, image 29), slightly decreased or at the limits of measurement variability; stable to marginally improved.
- Right middle lobe nodule 2 (previously 0.3 cm): now measures 0.2 cm (series 3, image 31); stable to minimally decreased, at the threshold of reliable measurement.
- Two additional bilateral subcentimeter nodules measuring less than 0.3 cm, previously characterized as indeterminate: both stable in size and appearance, without new features to suggest progression. These remain below reliable RECIST measurement threshold.
- No new pulmonary nodules identified on careful review of lung windows and MIP reconstructions.
- No ground-glass opacities or consolidation to suggest infectious or inflammatory etiology.

Pleural Spaces: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Pericardium and Mediastinum: No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No anterior mediastinal mass. Aortic arch and great vessels appear unremarkable. No thymic mass.

Heart: Cardiac silhouette within normal limits for technique. No gross valvular calcification.

Chest Wall and Osseous Thorax: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions within the visualized ribs, sternum, or thoracic vertebral bodies. No pathological fractures.

---

ABDOMEN:

Nephrectomy Bed / Prior Surgical Site: Status post right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. The right nephrectomy bed demonstrates expected postoperative changes including fibrofatty replacement without discrete soft tissue mass. No evidence of local recurrence. Surgical clips are noted in the right retroperitoneum at the expected location of the prior vascular pedicle and resected hilar lymph node. No new fluid collection or hematoma.

Right Adrenal Gland: Resected at time of prior surgery. No soft tissue at the right adrenal bed.

Left Kidney: Compensatory hypertrophy of the left kidney, measuring 14.1 cm in craniocaudal dimension. Normal cortical thickness and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi. No left renal mass. Left adrenal gland unremarkable.

Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes: The previously identified right paracaval lymph node, which measured 1.1 cm at cabozantinib initiation and subsequently decreased to 0.6 cm on the 6-month cabozantinib scan, is no longer reliably measurable as a discrete lymph node on this examination. There is a small residual soft tissue density at the right paracaval station measuring less than 0.5 cm and indistinguishable from a small reactive node or fibrotic change — this represents near-complete imaging response of this site. No new retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No aortocaval or left para-aortic lymph nodes meeting size criteria for pathological enlargement.

Liver: The liver is normal in size, contour, and attenuation. No hepatic lesions. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation. Portal vein patent with normal hepatopetal flow on portal venous phase imaging.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: Gallbladder present without cholelithiasis or wall thickening. Common bile duct not dilated.

Spleen: Normal size and homogeneous enhancement. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Pancreatic body and tail are visualized and appear unremarkable. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic stranding.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Stomach is decompressed and unremarkable. Small bowel and large bowel appear within normal limits without obstruction, wall thickening, or pneumoperitoneum. No mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

Mesentery and Peritoneum: No peritoneal nodularity or ascites. No omental caking.

Vasculature: Infrarenal aorta and iliac vessels without aneurysmal dilatation. No significant atherosclerotic disease beyond expected for age.

Abdominal Wall: No hernia identified.

---

PELVIS:

Uterus and Ovaries: Uterus is normal in size for a postmenopausal patient. No adnexal masses.

Urinary Bladder: Bladder is partially distended. No filling defects or wall irregularity.

Lymph Nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No inguinal lymphadenopathy meeting size criteria.

Osseous Pelvis: No lytic, sclerotic, or mixed osseous lesions within the visualized iliac wings, sacrum, or proximal femora. No pathological fractures.

---

SKELETAL SURVEY (limited, as visualized on CT):

No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions identified throughout the visualized axial and appendicular skeleton, including the thoracic and lumbar vertebral bodies, ribs, sternum, pelvis, and proximal femora. No pathological compression fractures. This is consistent with the recent MRI lumbar spine, which demonstrated degenerative disc disease without evidence of osseous metastatic disease.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued stable disease on cabozantinib with a sustained and favorable slow response across all pulmonary sites of disease. The dominant right lower lobe nodule measures approximately 2.0 cm, a slight interval decrease from 2.1 cm on the prior 6-month cabozantinib restaging scan, and is substantially reduced from its peak measurement of 2.6 cm at cabozantinib initiation.

2. The remaining bilateral pulmonary nodules — including the second right lower lobe nodule (0.4 cm), left lower lobe nodule (0.3 cm), and right middle lobe nodules (0.3 cm and 0.2 cm) — are grossly stable to very slightly decreased, consistent with an ongoing modest but durable treatment effect.

3. The previously identified right paracaval lymph node metastasis has achieved near-complete imaging response, no longer measurable as a discrete pathological node. This represents a meaningful treatment response at this site.

4. No new pulmonary nodules, no new retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and no new sites of extrapulmonary metastatic disease identified.

5. No hepatic, adrenal, or osseous metastases. No intracranial disease assessment on this examination (dedicated brain imaging deferred to prior MRI).

6. Status post right radical nephrectomy with no evidence of local recurrence at the nephrectomy bed.

Overall, this examination demonstrates continued durable stable disease with slow ongoing partial response to cabozantinib across all measurable and non-measurable disease sites. No evidence of progressive disease on this examination.
